question: Who are our top 20% of customers by total spend in the past 12 months, and what channels did they originally come from?
context: The CRM and loyalty team wants to identify high-value customers to target with a VIP retention program. Understanding which acquisition channels produce the most valuable long-term customers will also help the marketing team allocate budget more effectively. This analysis is the foundation for a tiered loyalty programme.
business_logic: Total spend per customer is calculated as the sum of net sales over the past 12 months, excluding returns. Customers are then ranked by total spend, and the top 20% by spend percentile are identified using a percentile cutoff. For each high-value customer, their original acquisition channel is pulled from the customer dimension. Results are grouped by acquisition channel to show which source produces the highest-value customers, and individual customer records are also returned for CRM upload. Only customers with at least two purchases in the period are included to exclude one-time buyers.
sql:
WITH customer_spend AS (
  SELECT
    s.customer_id,
    SUM(s.net_sales) AS total_spend_12m,
    COUNT(DISTINCT s.sale_id) AS total_transactions
  FROM fact_sales s
  JOIN dim_date d ON s.date_id = d.date_id
  WHERE s.is_return = FALSE
    AND d.full_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '12 months'
  GROUP BY s.customer_id
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT s.sale_id) >= 2
),
ranked_customers AS (
  SELECT
    cs.customer_id,
    cs.total_spend_12m,
    cs.total_transactions,
    NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY cs.total_spend_12m DESC) AS spend_quintile
  FROM customer_spend cs
)
SELECT
  rc.customer_id,
  c.customer_segment,
  c.acquisition_channel,
  c.region,
  rc.total_spend_12m,
  rc.total_transactions,
  ROUND(rc.total_spend_12m / rc.total_transactions, 2) AS avg_order_value
FROM ranked_customers rc
JOIN dim_customer c ON rc.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE rc.spend_quintile = 1
ORDER BY rc.total_spend_12m DESC;
